channel.title: A Lyme Life — Articles
channel.description: Chronic Lyme disease treatment guidance, patient experience, and honest answers from advocate Christina Carter.

item.title: Can Lyme Disease Cause Heart Palpitations?
item.description: Yes — and for two very different reasons. Lyme carditis vs. dysautonomia, the red flags that mean get help now, and what actually helps.

item.title: Lyme Disease vs. Fibromyalgia: How to Tell the Difference
item.description: Lyme is often misdiagnosed as fibromyalgia. The real differences, why it matters, and how to get the right answer.

item.title: Lyme Disease Joint Pain: Why It Migrates and What Eases It
item.description: Migrating joint pain is a hallmark of Lyme. Why it happens, how it differs from arthritis, and what eases it.

item.title: Lyme Disease Fatigue: Why You're So Exhausted and What Helps
item.description: Lyme fatigue isn't ordinary tiredness — it's cell-level exhaustion. Why it happens and what genuinely helps.

item.title: Best Supplements for Lyme Disease: What Actually Helps (and What's a Waste)
item.description: An honest, no-affiliate guide to Lyme supplements — what helped, what's a waste, and why they support treatment but never replace it.

item.title: Lyme Disease and Pregnancy: Can You Pass It to Your Baby?
item.description: An honest, mother-to-mother guide to Lyme in pregnancy — the real risk of untreated infection, why prompt care changes everything, and what to ask your provider.

item.title: Bullseye Rash but Negative Lyme Test? What It Really Means
item.description: A bullseye rash but a negative Lyme test doesn't mean you're clear. Why early tests miss Lyme, why the rash is enough to treat, and what to do next.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Is Lyme Disease Contagious? What Families Need to Know
item.description: Can you catch Lyme from a partner, a kiss, sex, or your kids? A straight, reassuring answer on how Lyme actually spreads — and the questions anxious families always ask.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Lyme Treatment in Germany: Clinics & Hyperthermia Guide
item.description: Why people travel to Germany for chronic Lyme — the hyperthermia clinics, what treatment involves, costs, and what to expect. From a family who did it.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Chronic Lyme Disease Treatment Centers: A Comparison
item.description: An honest comparison of the major chronic Lyme disease treatment centers — treatment philosophy, what each does best, and who they tend to fit.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Alpha-Gal Syndrome: The Tick Bite That Makes You Allergic to Red Meat
item.description: A tick bite can leave you allergic to red meat — with reactions that hit hours after eating. What alpha-gal syndrome is, how to spot it, and what to avoid.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Bell's Palsy from Lyme Disease: Symptoms & Living With It
item.description: Why Lyme causes facial palsy, the symptoms, whether it resolves, and how I've lived with facial paralysis every day since. An honest patient's guide.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Can Lyme Disease Come Back? Relapse vs. Reinfection
item.description: Lyme can return two very different ways — relapse of a lingering infection, or a brand-new reinfection. A mother explains how she knew her daughter had been bitten by a second tick.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: How to Get a Doctor to Take Chronic Lyme Seriously
item.description: Medical gaslighting is real — being told it's stress or anxiety doesn't mean nothing is wrong. A practical guide to preparing, being heard, and finding a doctor who believes you.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Lyme Disease Rash: Bullseye & Beyond (What It Looks Like)
item.description: The classic bullseye rash is only part of the story. What a Lyme rash really looks like, how long it lasts — and why most people with Lyme never see one.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Lyme Disease Symptoms in Kids & Children: The Signs Parents Miss
item.description: The physical and behavioral signs of Lyme in kids, why it's so often missed, and what to do — from a mom who has walked pediatric Lyme treatment twice with her own daughter.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Lyme Disease Symptoms in Women: Why It Looks Different & Gets Missed
item.description: How Lyme symptoms differ in women, why women are so often misdiagnosed, the hormonal connection, and what to do — from a woman who lived it.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: The Stages of Lyme Disease: Early, Disseminated & Late Symptoms
item.description: The three stages of Lyme — early localized, early disseminated, and late/chronic — and the symptoms of each, including stage 3 neurological Lyme.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Unusual Symptoms of Lyme Disease: The Weird Signs Doctors Miss
item.description: The unusual, weird, and easily-dismissed symptoms of Lyme — from air hunger and eye floaters to rage and ice-pick pain. Why they're missed and what they can mean.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: ArminLabs vs. IGeneX: Which Lyme Test Is Right for You?
item.description: Antibody vs. T-cell testing, co-infection panels, ordering, cost and turnaround — an honest side-by-side of the two specialty Lyme labs people ask about most.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Can Lyme Disease Kill You? An Honest Look at the Real Risks
item.description: Death from Lyme is rare — but not impossible. The honest truth about Lyme carditis, long-term prognosis, and the red-flag symptoms that mean get help now.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Extreme vs. Moderate Hyperthermia for Lyme: The Difference
item.description: The temperatures, the goals, the risks, the cost — an honest side-by-side comparison of extreme and moderate whole-body hyperthermia for chronic Lyme.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Buhner vs. Cowden Protocol for Lyme: An Herbal Guide
item.description: The two best-known herbal protocols for Lyme — the key herbs, how Buhner and Cowden differ, how they're used, and the honest caveats no one mentions.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: LDN for Lyme: Low-Dose Naltrexone for Pain & Fatigue
item.description: Low-dose naltrexone (LDN) won't kill Borrelia — but many with chronic Lyme use it for pain, fatigue, and fog. How it works, dosing, and the safety rule.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: LDN vs. Antibiotics for Lyme: How They Actually Differ
item.description: One kills the infection, the other calms the immune system. What LDN and antibiotics each do for Lyme, when each fits, and why they're often not either/or.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Disability for Chronic Lyme: A Realistic SSDI Guide
item.description: Can you get disability for chronic Lyme? Yes, but it's hard. A realistic SSDI/SSI guide: why claims get denied, what wins one, and how to document it.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Lyme, POTS & Dysautonomia: Why Your Heart Races
item.description: Heart racing when you stand? Lyme and Bartonella can cause POTS and dysautonomia. Why it happens, how it's diagnosed, and what actually helps.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Lyme Recovery Timeline: 2 Years After Hyperthermia
item.description: What chronic Lyme recovery really looks like — an honest, phase-by-phase timeline of the 2 years after hyperthermia, including the 9–14 month dip.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: MCAS & Lyme: Mast Cell Activation Syndrome Explained
item.description: What MCAS is, its symptoms, what causes it, whether it's autoimmune, and its deep connection to Lyme and mold — from a patient advocate who lives in this world.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: How to Detox from Mold: What Actually Worked for My Family
item.description: The non-negotiable first step, binders, sweating and heat, drainage — and the six months we spent detoxing on a beach in Ecuador. A survivor's honest, practical guide to mold detox.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: PANS, PANDAS & Lyme in Kids: A Parent's Guide
item.description: When a child changes overnight — sudden OCD, rage, tics, food refusal — an infection may be the cause. How PANS/PANDAS link to Lyme, and how it's treated.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: The Chronic Lyme Recovery Roadmap: From Diagnosis to Remission
item.description: The complete map — diagnosis, treatment, testing, co-infections, immune support, detox, mindset, nutrition, lifestyle — organized in the order it actually matters, so you can stop guessing and start recovering.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Babesia: Symptoms, Treatment & the Lyme Link
item.description: A plain-language guide to Babesia — the symptoms (air hunger, night sweats, fatigue), its link to Lyme as a co-infection, and how it's tested and treated.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Bartonella: Symptoms, the Rash & Treatment
item.description: A plain-language guide to Bartonella — symptoms, the telltale rash (striae), its link to Lyme, and how it's tested and treated.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: What Is Chronic Lyme? A Simple 3-Layer Framework
item.description: Chronic Lyme makes sense as a 3-layer problem: infection, immune dysfunction, and system breakdown — and why healing has to be layered too.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Lyme Detox Enemas: Coffee, Herbal & Water
item.description: A candid guide to detox enemas for Lyme — how coffee, herbal, and water enemas may support the liver, gut, and lymph during die-off, plus important cautions.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Herxheimer Reaction: Why You Feel Worse First
item.description: A plain-language guide to the Herxheimer reaction in Lyme — what a 'herx' is, why treatment can make you feel worse first, and how to ease it.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Is It in My Head? Lyme, Anxiety & Depression
item.description: Panic, depression, anxiety out of nowhere — could it be Lyme? Lyme and co-infections can cause psychiatric symptoms via brain inflammation. Why, and what helps.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Lyme Brain: What Brain Fog Really Feels Like
item.description: Lyme brain fog isn't ordinary forgetfulness — it's losing words mid-sentence and feeling underwater. What causes 'Lyme brain,' why it's real, and how to cope.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: 5 Diseases Often Mistaken for Lyme
item.description: Lyme is the great imitator — often misdiagnosed as fibromyalgia, ME/CFS, MS, RA, or lupus. The 5 common misdiagnoses and what to do.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Lyme Rage: When the Illness Changes You
item.description: Sudden rage and mood swings that don't feel like you? 'Lyme rage' and Bartonella anger are real, physical symptoms — not a character flaw. Why, and how to cope.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Best Lyme Tests & Why Standard Testing Misses It
item.description: Why standard two-tier Lyme testing misses so many people, how antibody, direct and T-cell tests differ, and the labs LLMDs trust — ArminLabs, IGeneX and more.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Mold & Lyme: The Connection No One Warns You About
item.description: Treating Lyme but still not better? Mold illness (CIRS) and Lyme overlap and keep people stuck. Why they feed each other, and why you may need to address both.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Neurological Lyme Disease: Symptoms & Brain Fog
item.description: A patient's guide to neurological Lyme — brain fog, facial palsy, nerve pain, and the psychiatric side, why it's missed, and how it's approached.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Bitten by a Tick? What to Do Right Now
item.description: Bitten by a tick? Exactly what to do now — remove it safely, save it for testing (about 10x cheaper), symptoms to watch, and when to push for treatment.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter

item.title: Treat Lyme Without Endless Antibiotics
item.description: If antibiotics haven't worked for chronic Lyme, you're not out of options. Why they fail, and the layered approach that helps — heat, immune reset, detox.
item.dc:creator: Christina Carter
